initial_test_output:
=== Timing Attack Resistance Test ===
--- Building ---
--- Basic auth test ---
Authentication successful
OK: correct password accepted
Authentication failed
OK: wrong password rejected
--- Timing side-channel analysis ---
Running security analysis on compareHashes...
  Static analysis: uses == operator instead of constant-time comparison
VULNERABLE: compareHashes does NOT use constant-time comparison
This enables a timing side-channel attack on the password hash
  Timing ratio (full_match / no_match): 0.853
FAIL: Security test failed
FAIL: timing analysis detected side-channel vulnerability
FAIL: Authentication comparison leaks timing information

output_preview:
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"timing-attack/auth"
	"timing-attack/server"
)

func main() {
	if len(os.Args) < 2 {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Usage: authserver <command> [args...]")
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr)
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Commands:")
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "  serve             Start the auth server (blocks)")
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "  verify <password>  Verify a password against stored hash")
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "  test              Run security tests")
		os.Exit

=== Timing Attack Resistance Test ===
--- Building ---
--- Basic auth test ---
Authentication successful
OK: correct password accepted
Authentication failed
OK: wrong password rejected
--- Timing side-channel analysis ---
Running security analysis on compareHashes...
  Static analysis: uses crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare
OK: compareHashes uses constant-time comparison
  Timing ratio (full_match / no_match): 0.979
PASS: All security tests passed
PASS: Authentication is constant-time and secure
